title: Ctenitopsis trichotoma
taxonomy: Ctenitopsis trichotoma is a species of flowering plant in the fern family Dryopteridaceae. It is a type of fern that belongs to the genus Ctenitopsis. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central America. The classification of Ctenitopsis trichotoma is further broken down into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species levels. Ctenitopsis trichotoma's classification is a way to identify and categorize the plant within the vast diversity of plant life.

intro: Ctenitopsis trichotoma is a species of plant in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as a fern. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitopsis trichotoma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
